title: Incorrect Count of Leading Zeros in Binary Representation

statement.markdown:
Given a positive integer n, count the number of leading zeros in binary representation of n. If n is 0, return 0.

You should not use any built-in functions for binary representation.
statement.outputFormat: An integer representing the count of leading zeros.
statement.notes: Be careful of edge cases and overflow.

examples.explanation: Binary representation of 4 is 100, which has 2 leading zeros.
Binary representation of 8 is 1000, which has 1 leading zero.
There are no leading zeros in binary representation of 0.
